extraction.one_line_summary: KING Ben won from a 10-minute breakaway on a 12km climb after forcing selections while ROLLAND Pierre did too much chase work and faded.
tactical_patterns: KING Ben pre-selected this stage from route book, rode climbing bike not aero, forced breakaway fragmentation before final climb.
Team Sky failed to control despite KWIATKOWSKI Michał in leader's jersey, appeared without GC depth or three-week plan.
YATES Simon counter-attacked after Lotto setup pulled back LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel, exploiting Sky's isolation of KWIATKOWSKI.
BUCHMANN Emanuel now second overall with strong GC support (MAJKA, FORMOLO), potentially widest-open Grand Tour post-Sky dominance.

rider_signals.KING Ben: positive: Identifies breakaway-friendly stages from reconnaissance, forces selection timing before companions recover.
rider_signals.ROLLAND Pierre: negative: Strongest climber in break but exhausted himself dragging group back, attacked too late.
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: negative: Team Sky left him isolated in leader's jersey with no domestique support on GC climb.
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Counter-attacked brilliantly off Lotto setup, still has Giro-winning form despite later fade there.
rider_signals.BUCHMANN Emanuel: positive: Few seconds behind YATES with MAJKA Rafał and FORMOLO Davide supporting, potential breakout Grand Tour.
rider_signals.BENNETT George: negative: Failed to respond when YATES attacked despite having KUSS Sepp support riding for him.
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: negative: Already 16 minutes down, clearly using Vuelta as extended training camp for Worlds.
